html:
[1210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 阿足师 - A title or name, likely referring to a monk or spiritual figure.
  • 形质痴浊 - Describes someone as physically dull or spiritually muddled.
  • 神情不慧 - Indicates a lack of mental clarity or wisdom.
  • 靡不先觉 - Means 'nothing escapes his foresight' or 'he has no difficulty predicting things.'
  • 居虽无定 - Means 'his residence is not fixed' or 'he does not have a permanent home.'
  • 多寓阌乡 - Means 'he often stays in Wenxiang' (a place name).
  • 憧憧往来 - Describes someone who is constantly moving or bustling about.
  • 争路礼谒 - Means 'competing for paths and showing respect' (possibly referring to people vying for his attention or blessings).
  • 檀施曾不顾瞻 - Means 'donations and offerings were never refused' or 'he did not turn away from giving.'
  • 或有谓曰 - Means 'someone said' or 'it was said.'
  • 投告希其痊除 - Means 'to submit a request for his healing or cure.'
  • 致斋 - Means 'to perform a ritual purification' or 'to prepare for a religious ceremony.'
  • 广召众多 - Means 'to widely invite many people.'
  • 同观度脱 - Means 'to observe together and be liberated' (possibly referring to spiritual liberation).
  • 赍致其男 - Means 'to bring his son' or 'to present his son.'
  • 道场 - Refers to a place of religious practice or meditation.
  • 神通 - Refers to supernatural abilities or powers.
  • 遽此解挥 - Means 'to quickly resolve or dismiss.'
  • 傥或不然 - Means 'if not so' or 'unless otherwise.'
  • 都不愚痴 - Means 'not at all foolish' or 'completely not stupid.'
  • 沈水不知所适 - Means 'to sink into the water and lose track of where to go' (possibly referring to the son's disappearance).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 阿足师 - The identity of '阿足师' is unclear, but he is likely a monk or spiritual figure with supernatural abilities.
  • 阌乡 - A place name, likely in the region of Shaanxi or Henan during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 陜州 - A historical region in China, now part of Henan Province.
  • 张臻 - A wealthy man mentioned in the text, possibly a fictional character or a historical figure.
  • 集异记 - A collection of strange or supernatural stories, often attributed to Tang Dynasty writers.

Proper Nouns

  • 阿足师 - A title or name, likely referring to a monk or spiritual figure.
  • 阌乡 - A place name, likely in the region of Shaanxi or Henan during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 陜州 - A historical region in China, now part of Henan Province.
  • 张臻 - A wealthy man mentioned in the text, possibly a fictional character or a historical figure.
  • 集异记 - A collection of strange or supernatural stories, often attributed to Tang Dynasty writers.

[1211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鉴其名者 - To recognize by name, indicating a form of acknowledgment or familiarity.
  • 负笈来告去 - Carrying books or scholarly equipment, often used to describe scholars traveling for study; to come and inform of departure.
  • 治装 - To prepare for a journey or to pack.
  • 道出灵岩寺下 - To pass by or travel through; Lingyan Temple is the name of a temple.
  • 群僧画像 - A group of monks; portraits.
  • 神降于我 - Divine presence or revelation; upon me.
  • 谨受敎 - To respectfully receive; instruction.
  • 卒年 - Died at the age of.
  • 七十八 - The specific age of death.
  • 冯氏子 - Surname is Feng, son.
  • 年十岁 - Ten years old.
  • 学浮图法 - Another term for Buddhism.
  • 以道行闻 - Known for his practice of the Way or known for his virtuous conduct.
  • 卒 - Died.
  • 冯阅其题益异之 - Feng looked at the inscription; found it even more extraordinary.
  • 益异之 - Found it even more extraordinary.
  • 鉴师 - The name of the monk.
  • 信士 - Faithful layperson.
  • 欺我 - To deceive me.
  • 异 - Extraordinary or strange.
  • 异人 - Extraordinary person or strange person.
  • 泣下 - To cry.
  • 题曰 - The inscription reads.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 侨居长安中 - To live as an exile or guest in another place; ancient name for Xi'an, the capital of the Tang dynasty.
  • 尉于东越 - Military or administrative official; ancient region in southeastern China.
  • 西庑下 - Under the west porch; side rooms or porches of a building.

Proper Nouns

  • 长安 - Ancient name for Xi'an, the capital of the Tang dynasty.
  • 东越 - Ancient region in southeastern China.
  • 灵岩寺 - Name of a temple.

[1212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 从谏 - The title of the text, meaning 'Following Advice.'
  • 东都敬爱寺北禅院 - Refers to the Northern Zen Hall of the Jing'ai Temple in Dongdu (Eastern Capital, i.e., Luoyang).
  • 姓张氏南阳人徙居广陵为土著姓 - A phrase that describes the monk's lineage and migration, meaning 'Originally from Nanyang, he moved to Guangling and became a native there.'
  • 越壮室之年忽顿悟真理 - Refers to a sudden enlightenment after passing his prime, meaning 'After passing his prime, he suddenly attained true understanding.'
  • 遂舍妻子从披削焉 - Describes the monk's decision to leave his family and enter monastic life, meaning 'He then left his wife and children and entered the monastic life.'
  • 研精禅观心境明白 - Describes the monk's deep study of Zen meditation and clear mind, meaning 'He devoted himself to Zen meditation, and his mind became clear.'
  • 及来洛遂止敬爱寺 - Describes the monk's arrival in Luoyang and his residence at the Jing'ai Temple, meaning 'When he came to Luoyang, he stayed at the Jing'ai Temple.'
  • 乃下郡国毁塔庙令沙门复初 - Refers to the emperor's decree to destroy Buddhist temples and return monks to secular life, meaning 'He ordered the destruction of Buddhist temples and the return of monks to lay life.'
  • 谏公夏日常于中入寂 - Refers to the monk's practice of entering meditation, meaning 'During the summer, he often entered meditation in this place.'
  • 诸兄走往林中谏公恬然趺坐若无所闻者 - Describes the monk's calm demeanor during the storm, meaning 'His brothers ran to the forest, but the monk remained calmly seated, as if he had not heard anything.'
  • 诸兄致问徐曰恶畜生而已 - Refers to the monk's response to his brothers' inquiries, meaning 'When his brothers asked him about it, he calmly replied, 'Just a bad animal.''
  • 至大中初宣宗复兴内敎谏公归东都故居其 - Describes the monk's return to Luoyang after the revival of Buddhism, meaning 'When Emperor Xuānzōng revived Buddhism at the beginning of the Dàzhōng era, the monk returned to his former home in Dongdu.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 东都 - Refers to Luoyang, the Eastern Capital of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 敬爱寺 - A Buddhist temple in Luoyang.
  • 北禅院 - The Northern Zen Hall of the temple.
  • 从谏 - The name of the monk.
  • 张氏 - The surname of the monk.
  • 南阳 - A region in Henan Province.
  • 广陵 - A region in Jiangsu Province.
  • 土著姓 - Refers to becoming a native of a place.
  • 八尺 - A unit of measurement, approximately 2.4 meters.
  • 壮室之年 - Refers to the prime of life, around 30-40 years old.
  • 顿悟真理 - Refers to sudden enlightenment in Buddhism.
  • 披削 - Refers to entering monastic life.
  • 禅观 - Refers to Zen meditation.
  • 缁黄 - Refers to monks (缁) and laypeople (黄).
  • 唐武宗 - The 15th emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 墨降龙之敎 - Refers to the reduction of Buddhist influence.
  • 郡国 - Refers to prefectures and kingdoms.
  • 塔庙 - Refers to Buddhist temples.
  • 沙门 - Refers to monks.
  • 乌帽麻衣 - Refers to a black hat and coarse clothing.
  • 皇甫枚 - A Tang Dynasty writer.
  • 温泉别业 - Refers to a hot spring villa.
  • 乔木骈郁 - Refers to tall, dense trees.
  • 巨石砥平 - Refers to large, flat stones.
  • 入寂 - Refers to entering meditation.
  • 补毳事 - Refers to mending a robe.
  • 霆击 - Refers to a lightning strike.
  • 大檀 - Refers to a large tree.
  • 诸兄 - Refers to the monk's brothers.
  • 恬然趺坐 - Refers to calmly seated in the lotus position.
  • 恶畜生 - Refers to a bad animal.
  • 大中初 - Refers to the beginning of the Dàzhōng era.
  • 宣宗 - The 18th emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 复兴内敎 - Refers to the revival of Buddhism.
  • 东都故居 - Refers to the monk's former home in Luoyang.

[1213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 子自广陵来观适与遇于院门 - Narrative introduction setting the scene of a chance encounter.
  • 威貌崇严不复可识 - Describes the person's dignified and imposing appearance, which has changed so much that he is no longer recognizable.
  • 乃拜而问 - Indicates a formal act of bowing and asking questions.
  • 从谏大徳所居 - Refers to the residence of a virtuous advisor.
  • 近东头 - A direction indicating proximity.
  • 遂阖门不出 - Describes the action of closing the door and not leaving.
  • 割裂亲爱又如此 - Describes the separation of close family members.
  • 咸通丙戌岁夏五月 - A specific date in the Tang dynasty calendar.
  • 忽遍诣所信向家皆谓曰 - Describes a sudden visit to trusted families, where the person says something.
  • 善建福业 - Means 'to build good karma' or 'to cultivate merit.'
  • 贫道秋初当逺行 - Means 'I, a poor monk, will be traveling far in the early autumn.'
  • 故相别耳 - Means 'so we bid farewell.'
  • 至秋七月朔清旦盥手焚香念慈氏如来 - Describes a ritual act of washing hands, burning incense, and reciting the name of the Buddha Maitreya.
  • 遂右脇而卧 - Describes the act of lying down on the right side.
  • 人生难得恶道易沦 - Means 'human life is hard to come by, while falling into evil realms is easy.'
  • 唯有归命释尊励精梵行 - Means 'only by devoting oneself to the Buddha and striving for pure conduct.'
  • 龙花防上当复相逢 - Means 'we will meet again in the future.'
  • 生也有涯与尔少别 - Means 'life is limited, and I am leaving you for a short time.'
  • 是日无疾奄化 - Means 'on that day, without any illness, he passed away.'
  • 年有八十余矣 - Means 'he was over eighty years old.'
  • 施诸鸟兽三日复视之肌貌如生无物敢近 - Describes the state of the body after three days, still appearing lifelike with no animals daring to approach.
  • 覆以饼饵经宿有狼狐迹唯啗饼饵而丰肤宛然 - Describes the wolves and foxes eating the offerings, leaving the body intact.
  • 乃依天竺法阇维讫收余烬起白塔于道傍 - Describes the cremation according to Indian Buddhist customs and the erection of a white stupa.
  • 春秋奉香火之荐焉 - Means 'yearly offerings of incense and fire were made.'
  • 出三水小牍 - Indicates the source of the text.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 广陵 - A city in present-day Jiangsu Province, known for its cultural significance.
  • 咸通丙戌岁 - The year 866 AD during the Tang dynasty.
  • 慈氏如来 - Refers to the Buddha Maitreya, the future Buddha.
  • 建春门 - A gate in the city of Chang'an, the capital of the Tang dynasty.
  • 尸陁林 - A forest where bodies are left for animals to consume, a practice in Buddhist funerary rites.
  • 阇维 - Cremation according to Indian Buddhist customs.
  • 白塔 - A white stupa, a type of Buddhist monument.
  • 三水小牍 - A collection of historical anecdotes and biographies from the Tang dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 广陵 - A city in present-day Jiangsu Province.
  • 咸通丙戌岁 - The year 866 AD.
  • 慈氏如来 - The Buddha Maitreya.
  • 建春门 - A gate in Chang'an.
  • 尸陁林 - A forest for funerary rites.
  • 阇维 - Cremation.
  • 白塔 - A white stupa.
  • 三水小牍 - A historical text.

[1214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太平广记 - A comprehensive collection of stories and anecdotes from ancient China, often used to illustrate moral or historical lessons.
  • 钦定四库全书 - A massive compilation of Chinese literature commissioned by the Qing dynasty, signifying the official endorsement of the text.
  • 异僧十二 - A section or chapter within the *太平广记* focusing on stories about extraordinary monks.
  • 结坛设佛像 - A ritual practice involving the establishment of an altar and the placement of a Buddha statue, often associated with divination or spiritual guidance.
  • 灾戾未已 - A phrase indicating that misfortune or calamity has not yet ceased.
  • 万里南去 - A euphemism for a significant journey or exile, often implying a long and arduous path.
  • 南行之期不旬月矣 - A statement indicating that the time for the journey to the south is within a few months.
  • 然则吾师何以明其不妄 - A rhetorical question asking how the teacher can demonstrate that their predictions are not false.
  • 陈目前事为验 - A phrase meaning to demonstrate the truth of the prediction by referring to current events.
  • 果有说也 - A phrase indicating that there is indeed something to be said or proven.
  • 石函 - A stone box or container, often used in ancient rituals or to hold important items.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太子少保 - A high-ranking official position in the imperial court, often held by trusted advisors to the crown prince.
  • 齐州僧 - A monk from Qi Prefecture, though the specific identity of this monk is not provided in the text.
  • 抱玉师 - A monk or teacher named Baoyu, whose specific background is not detailed in the text.
  • 束草师 - A monk or teacher named Shucao, whose specific background is not detailed in the text.
  • 惠寛 - A monk or teacher named Huikuan, whose specific background is not detailed in the text.
  • 素和尚 - A monk named Su, though the specific identity of this monk is not provided in the text.
  • 懐信 - A monk or teacher named Huai Xin, whose specific background is not detailed in the text.
  • 佛陁萨 - A term that could refer to a Buddhist monk or teacher, though the specific identity is not provided in the text.
  • 兴元上座 - A monk or teacher associated with the city of Xingyuan, though the specific identity is not detailed in the text.
  • 赵蕃 - A monk or teacher named Zhao Fan, whose specific background is not detailed in the text.
  • 懐濬 - A monk or teacher named Huai Jun, whose specific background is not detailed in the text.
  • 智者禅师法本 - A monk or teacher named Zhizhe, whose specific background is not detailed in the text.

Proper Nouns

  • 太平广记 - The title of the collection of stories and anecdotes.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The official endorsement of the text.
  • 异僧十二 - The specific section of the *太平广记* focusing on extraordinary monks.
  • 齐州僧 - The name of a monk from Qi Prefecture.
  • 抱玉师 - The name of a monk or teacher.
  • 束草师 - The name of a monk or teacher.
  • 惠寛 - The name of a monk or teacher.
  • 素和尚 - The name of a monk.
  • 懐信 - The name of a monk or teacher.
  • 佛陁萨 - The name of a monk or teacher.
  • 兴元上座 - The name of a monk or teacher.
  • 赵蕃 - The name of a monk or teacher.
  • 懐濬 - The name of a monk or teacher.
  • 智者禅师法本 - The name of a monk or teacher.

[1215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 平生當食萬羊 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'one should eat ten thousand sheep in a lifetime,' likely referring to a life of luxury or indulgence.
  • 今食九千五百矣 - Literally 'has eaten nine thousand five hundred,' indicating that the person has consumed a significant amount of sheep.
  • 所以當還者未盡五百羊耳 - Concessive phrase meaning 'there are still five hundred sheep left to be eaten,' suggesting that the person's indulgence is not yet complete.
  • 吾師果至人 - Phrase meaning 'my teacher is indeed a sage,' indicating respect and admiration for the teacher.
  • 且我元和十三年為丞相張公從事於北都 - Temporal clause meaning 'and in the 13th year of the Yuanhe era, I served as an assistant to Chancellor Zhang in the northern capital,' providing historical context.
  • 嘗夢行於晉山 - Phrase meaning 'I once dreamed of walking in Jin Mountain,' introducing a dream narrative.
  • 見山上盡目皆羊 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'I saw that the entire mountain was covered with sheep,' emphasizing the abundance of sheep in the dream.
  • 有牧者十數迎拜我 - Phrase meaning 'there were several shepherds who greeted and bowed to me,' indicating a sense of respect or awe.

[1216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 诀往乃依僧解衣载之而浮登岸 - Sequence of actions: going forth, relying on a monk, removing clothes, floating to the shore.
  • 论与僧各食一蔕腹饱矣 - Monk and scholar each ate one peach and felt full.
  • 论解衣将尽力包之 - Scholar removed clothes and tried their best to carry something.
  • 僧切戒论不言论 - Monk sternly warned not to speak of it.
  • 论亦疑生非常取两颗而返 - Scholar suspected something unusual and took two peaches.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 长安 - Ancient capital of China.
  • 菩提寺 - Buddhist temple.
  • 庆云 - Cloud of auspiciousness, associated with good fortune and divine favor.
  • 香乳 - Fragrant milk, often used in religious rituals.
  • 祥光 - Blessing light, associated with divine presence.
  • 佛 - Buddha, founder of Buddhism.
  • 束草师 - Monk known as 'Bundle of Grass Master,' who is said to have burned himself to death, leaving only ashes.

Proper Nouns

  • 长安 - Chang'an, ancient capital of China.
  • 菩提寺 - Bodhi Temple, Buddhist temple.
  • 庆云 - Cloud of auspiciousness.
  • 香乳 - Fragrant milk.
  • 祥光 - Blessing light.
  • 佛 - Buddha.
  • 束草师 - Bundle of Grass Master.

[1217]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 黄箓斋 - A specific type of Buddhist ritual, often translated as 'Yellow Scripture Rite' or 'Yellow Scripture Ceremony.'
  • 和尚蕈 - A type of mushroom that grows near the temple, named after the monk 'Hui Kuan.' It can be translated as 'Monk's Mushroom.'
  • 法华经 - Refers to the Lotus Sutra, a significant Mahayana Buddhist scripture.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 石天尊像 - The Jade Emperor is a central figure in Taoist cosmology, often depicted as a stone statue in temples.
  • 元和中 - Refers to the reign period of Emperor Xianzong of Tang (806-820 AD).
  • 昭国郑相 - Refers to a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty, specifically a chancellor from the state of Zhao.
  • 酉阳杂俎 - A collection of strange stories and anecdotes compiled by Li Fang during the Tang Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 惠寛 - Monk Hui Kuan.
  • 绵州浄慧寺 - A Buddhist temple located in Mianzhou (modern-day Mianyang, Sichuan Province).
  • 长安兴善寺 - A temple in Chang'an (modern-day Xi'an).
  • 素和尚 - Master Su.

[1218]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 白雨如泻 - Vivid metaphor describing heavy rain, literally 'white rain like a waterfall.'
  • 漆其尸而瘗焉 - Means 'to lacquer the body and bury it,' a specific ritual practice.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 扬州西灵塔 - Refers to a specific pagoda in Yangzhou, a famous cultural and historical site.
  • 唐武宗末 - Refers to the end of Emperor Wuzong's reign, a significant period in Tang Dynasty history.
  • 淮南词客刘隐之 - Refers to Liu Yin, a poet from Huainan, a region in eastern China.
  • 佛陀萨 - A name that could be a transliteration of a Buddhist term or a personal name, likely referring to a monk or religious figure.
  • 岐阳法门寺 - Refers to a specific temple in Qiyang, known for its historical and religious significance.
  • 开成五年 - A specific year in the reign of Emperor Wenzong of Tang, corresponding to 840 CE.

Proper Nouns

  • 扬州西灵塔 - A specific pagoda in Yangzhou.
  • 唐武宗 - Emperor Wuzong of Tang.
  • 淮南词客刘隐之 - Liu Yin, a poet from Huainan.
  • 明州 - An ancient name for a region in China.
  • 东海 - The East China Sea.
  • 开成五年 - 840 CE, a specific year in the reign of Emperor Wenzong of Tang.
  • 岐阳法门寺 - A specific temple in Qiyang.
  • 佛陀萨 - A name that could be a transliteration of a Buddhist term or a personal name.

[1219]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 興元上座 - Title or position, likely referring to a high-ranking monk or abbot in the Xingyuan area.
  • 常飲酒食肉 - Literally 'often drinks wine and eats meat,' indicating a habit of indulgence.
  • 羣輩皆效焉 - 'The group all imitates him,' suggesting that others follow his behavior.
  • 多作大餅 - 'Makes many large cakes,' indicating a preparation of food.
  • 招羣徒眾 - 'Invites many disciples,' referring to gathering followers or students.
  • 入尸陁林 - 'Enters the cemetery,' a place where the dead are buried.
  • 以餅裹腐尸肉而食 - 'Wraps rotten corpse meat in the cake and eats it,' describing a macabre act.
  • 數啖不已 - 'Eats repeatedly without stopping,' indicating persistent behavior.
  • 掩鼻而走 - 'Cover their noses and run away,' a reaction to the stench or disgust.
  • 汝等能食此肉 - 'Can you all eat this meat,' a rhetorical question challenging the others.
  • 方可食諸肉 - 'Then you can eat all kinds of meat,' implying a test of resolve.
  • 自此缁徒因成精进也 - 'From then on, the monastic community became diligent,' indicating a change in behavior.
  • 唐国子祭酒 - A high-ranking official in the Tang dynasty, responsible for education and cultural affairs.
  • 太和七年 - 833 AD, a specific year in the Tang dynasty.
  • 南宫郎 - A position in the imperial bureaucracy, likely related to the palace.
  • 遺我裁刀一千五百 - 'Give me a thousand five hundred knives,' a request for a specific number of knives.
  • 庶可脱君之祸 - 'Perhaps it can save you from your calamity,' a conditional statement.
  • 不然未旬日 - 'Otherwise, within ten days,' indicating a short timeframe.
  • 东南一郡耳 - 'Just one prefecture in the southeast,' a specific geographical reference.
  • 青龙寺 - The name of a Buddhist temple.
  • 法安 - The name of a monk.
  • 约来日就送焉 - 'Agree to deliver them tomorrow,' indicating a promise to fulfill a request.
  • 访其名暨所居 - 'Ask for his name and residence,' indicating an inquiry.
  • 无影响踪迹 - 'No trace or sign,' indicating that the monk was not found.
  • 出为州刺史 - 'Was appointed as the governor of a prefecture,' indicating a change in official position.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 兴元县 - A county in the Xingyuan area, likely in present-day Shaanxi province.
  • 兰若 - A Buddhist monastery or hermitage.
  • 尸陁林 - A cemetery or burial ground, often associated with Buddhist practices.
  • 缁徒 - Refers to the monastic community, specifically Buddhist monks.
  • 精进 - Refers to diligence and effort in spiritual practice.
  • 国子祭酒 - A high-ranking official in the Tang dynasty, responsible for education and cultural affairs.
  • 太和七年 - 833 AD, a specific year in the Tang dynasty.
  • 南宫郎 - A position in the imperial bureaucracy, likely related to the palace.
  • 青龙寺 - A famous Buddhist temple in Chang'an (present-day Xi'an).
  • 法安 - The name of a monk, likely a historical figure.
  • 宣室志 - A historical text or record, likely containing accounts of historical events and figures.
  • 云溪友议 - A historical text or record, likely containing accounts of historical events and figures.

Proper Nouns

  • 兴元县 - A county in the Xingyuan area.
  • 兰若 - A Buddhist monastery or hermitage.
  • 尸陁林 - A cemetery or burial ground.
  • 缁徒 - The monastic community.
  • 国子祭酒 - A high-ranking official in the Tang dynasty.
  • 太和七年 - 833 AD.
  • 南宫郎 - A position in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 青龙寺 - A famous Buddhist temple in Chang'an.
  • 法安 - The name of a monk.
  • 宣室志 - A historical text or record.
  • 云溪友议 - A historical text or record.

[1220]

Idiomatic Expressions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 云但扣其柱即见其人 - 云 can be translated as 'said' or 'allegedly,' indicating a reported event. 但扣其柱 means 'merely strike the pillar,' and 即见其人 means 'and the person appears immediately.' The phrase suggests a supernatural or magical event where striking a pillar reveals someone.
  • 风雨四起 - A vivid expression meaning 'wind and rain arise in all directions,' indicating a sudden and dramatic change in the environment.
  • 咫尺莫窥 - 咫尺 means 'a short distance,' and 莫窥 means 'cannot be seen.' Together, this phrase suggests that despite being close, the scene is obscured.
  • 耳目割开 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'the ears and eyes are torn open,' suggesting a sudden and intense sensory experience.
  • 楼台对峙 - 楼台 refers to towers or pavilions, and 对峙 means 'face each other.' This phrase describes a scene where buildings are aligned opposite each other.
  • 身在三门之下 - 三门 refers to the three gates of a temple or monastery. This phrase places the narrator at the entrance of the temple.
  • 逡巡法本自内出而见之甚喜 - 逡巡 means 'hesitant' or 'cautiously,' and 法本 is likely the name of a monk. 自内出而见之甚喜 means 'came out from inside and was very happy to see him.' This describes a monk named Fa Ben emerging and greeting someone with joy.
  • 问南中之旧事 - 问 means 'ask,' and 南中 refers to the southern regions. 旧事 means 'old stories' or 'past events.' This phrase indicates that the monk asks about past events from the southern regions.
  • 引其僧度重门升袐殿叅其尊宿 - 引 means 'lead,' 度重门 means 'cross the heavy doors,' 升袐殿 means 'ascend the secret hall,' and 参其尊宿 means 'pay respects to the senior monk.' This describes a series of actions where the monk leads another monk through multiple doors and into a sacred hall to meet a senior monk.
  • 尊宿问其故 - 尊宿 refers to a senior monk, and 问其故 means 'ask the reason.' This phrase indicates that the senior monk inquires about the purpose of the visit.
  • 法本云早年相州同过夏期 - 法本 is the name of the monk, 云 means 'says,' 早年 means 'early years,' 相州 is a place name, and 同过夏期 means 'spent the summer together.' This phrase explains that the monk Fa Ben mentions they spent the summer together in Xiangzhou in the past.
  • 此相访故及山门也 - 此 means 'this,' 相访 means 'visit each other,' 故 means 'reason,' and 及山门 means 'up to the mountain gate.' This phrase explains that the reason for the visit is to meet at the mountain gate.
  • 可饭后请出在此无座位食毕法本送至山门相别 - 可 means 'can,' 饭后 means 'after the meal,' 请出 means 'invite out,' 在此无座位 means 'there is no seat here,' 食毕 means 'after eating,' 送至山门 means 'send to the mountain gate,' and 相别 means 'part ways.' This describes the sequence of events after the meal, where Fa Ben is invited out, there is no seat, and they part ways at the mountain gate.
  • 顷之宛在竹丛中石柱之侧 - 顷之 means 'soon after,' 宛在 means 'as if in,' 竹丛中 means 'in the bamboo grove,' and 石柱之侧 means 'beside the stone pillar.' This phrase describes the scene where the narrator finds themselves in a bamboo grove near a stone pillar.
  • 余并莫覩 - 余 means 'I,' and 并莫覩 means 'also cannot see.' This phrase indicates that the narrator cannot see what is happening.
  • 即知圣贤之在世隐显难涯 - 即知 means 'then know,' 圣贤 means 'sages and virtuous people,' 在世 means 'in the world,' 隐显 means 'hidden and revealed,' and 难涯 means 'difficult to fathom.' This phrase suggests that the narrator realizes the mysterious nature of sages and virtuous people, who can be both hidden and revealed.
  • 岂金粟如来独能化见者乎 - 岂 means 'how,' 金粟如来 is a title for a Buddha, 独能 means 'only able,' and 化见者 means 'to transform and appear.' This phrase questions whether only the Golden Grain Buddha can transform and appear.

  • 云但扣其柱即见其人其僧乃以小杖扣柱数声乃风雨四起 - This sequence of events is tightly linked and must be read as a single action: the monk strikes the pillar, and immediately, wind and rain arise.
  • 咫尺莫窥俄然耳目割开楼台对峙身在三门之下 - This series of actions and observations must be read as a continuous sequence: despite being close, the scene is obscured, then the ears and eyes are torn open, and the buildings are aligned opposite each other.
  • 逡巡法本自内出而见之甚喜问南中之旧事 - This describes a series of actions: the narrator hesitates, then Fa Ben emerges and greets him with joy, and asks about past events from the southern regions.

  • 当王所居三间高大尽垂 - '当王所居' means 'at the king's residence,' '三间' means 'three rooms,' '高大' means 'tall and large,' '尽垂' means 'all hung down with curtains' ( is a character for a type of curtain or screen).
  • 思元至未进见 - '思元' is a proper name, '至' means 'arrived,' '未' means 'not yet,' and '进见' means 'to meet.'
  • 有一人金章紫绶形状甚贵 - '有一人' means 'there was a person,' '金章' means 'golden seal,' '紫绶' means 'purple sash,' '形状' means 'appearance,' and '甚贵' means 'very noble.'
  • 令投刺谒王 - '令' means 'ordered,' '投刺' means 'to present a card' (a form of introduction), and '谒王' means 'to pay respects to the king.'
  • 王召见思元随而进至楼下 - '王' means 'the king,' '召见' means 'summoned to see,' '思元' is a proper name, '随而进' means 'followed and entered,' and '至楼下' means 'to the foot of the stairs.'
  • 王命却 - '王' means 'the king,' '命' means 'ordered,' '却' means 'to pull back,' and '' means 'curtains.'
  • 召贵人登楼 - '召' means 'summoned,' '贵人' means 'noble person,' and '登楼' means 'to ascend the building.'
  • 贵人自阶陛方登 - '贵人' means 'noble person,' '自' means 'from,' '阶陛' means 'stairs,' and '方登' means 'just began to ascend.'
  • 王见起延至下 - '王' means 'the king,' '见' means 'saw,' '起' means 'rose,' '延' means 'invited,' and '至下' means 'to the foot of the curtains.'
  • 贵人拜王答拜 - '贵人' means 'noble person,' '拜' means 'bowed,' '王' means 'the king,' and '答拜' means 'returned the bow.'
  • 谓贵人曰 - '谓' means 'told,' '贵人' means 'noble person,' and '曰' means 'said.'
  • 今既来此即须置对 - '今' means 'now,' '既' means 'already,' '来此' means 'here,' '即' means 'must,' '须' means 'need,' and '置对' means 'to respond.'
  • 不审在生有何善事 - '不审' means 'do not know,' '在生' means 'in life,' '何' means 'what,' and '善事' means 'good deeds.'
  • 无 - '无' means 'none.'
  • 在生数十年既无善事又不忠孝 - '在生' means 'in life,' '数十年' means 'several decades,' '既' means 'already,' '无善事' means 'no good deeds,' '又' means 'and,' '不忠孝' means 'not loyal or filial.'
  • 今当奈何 - '今' means 'now,' '当' means 'should,' and '奈何' means 'what to do.'
  • 因嚬蹙曰 - '因' means 'then,' '嚬蹙' means 'frowning and frowning,' and '曰' means 'said.'
  • 可取所司处分 - '可' means 'can,' '取' means 'take,' '所司' means 'the responsible official,' and '处分' means 'to handle.'
  • 贵人辞下 - '贵人' means 'noble person,' '辞' means 'excused,' and '下' means 'down.'
  • 未 - '未' means 'not yet.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 思元 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical or fictional character.
  • 王 - Refers to a king or ruler.
  • 贵人 - Refers to a noble or high-ranking individual.
  • 金章紫绶 - Refers to a golden seal and purple sash, symbols of high rank and nobility in ancient China.
  • 城门数重防卫甚备 - Describes the elaborate and well-prepared defenses of a city gate, indicating a high level of security.
  • 高楼十间 - Refers to a tall building with ten rooms, suggesting a grand and imposing structure.
  •  - Refers to a type of curtain or screen, often used in traditional Chinese architecture to partition spaces.
  • 阶陛 - Refers to the steps leading to a building, often associated with official or ceremonial buildings.
  • 忠孝 - Refers to loyalty and filial piety, core values in Confucian ethics.

Proper Nouns

  • 思元 - A proper name.
  • 王 - A proper name for the king.
  • 贵人 - A proper name for the noble person.

[1245]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 数级忽有大黑风到前直吹贵人将去 - Sudden, powerful black wind that sweeps a distinguished person away. '数级' likely refers to a series of steps or a platform, and '贵人' means a noble or distinguished person.
  • 遥见贵人在黑风中吹其身忽长数丈而状隳壊或大或小 - Seeing the distinguished person being blown by the wind, growing to several zhang in size, and then deteriorating into various forms, some large and some small.
  • 渐渐逺去便失所在 - Gradually moving away and eventually disappearing from sight.
  • 可捻筹定之 - Using counting sticks (筹) to determine something, possibly a decision or a fate.
  • 留二人舍思元思元出殿门门西墙有门东向门外众僧数百持旛花迎思元云菩萨要见思元入院 - The king keeping two people and sending Siyuan out of the palace, where he encounters hundreds of monks holding banners and flowers, who tell him that a bodhisattva wants to see him in the courtyard.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 业风 - A Buddhist concept referring to the wind of karma, which is believed to influence one's fate and destiny.
  • 地狱 - A Buddhist concept referring to a place of suffering and punishment for those who have committed evil deeds.
  • 筹 - Counting sticks used in ancient China for calculations and decision-making.
  • 地藏菩萨 - A bodhisattva in Mahayana Buddhism known for his vow to save all beings from suffering, especially in hell.
  • 七宝 - Refers to seven precious materials, often used in Buddhist texts to describe the adornment of sacred places or objects.

Proper Nouns

  • 思元 - A person's name, likely a monk or a subject.
  • 地藏菩萨 - A bodhisattva in Mahayana Buddhism.
  • 七宝 - Refers to seven precious materials.

[1246]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此人去亦不乆 - This person has not been away for long.
  • 闻昔之梵音故流涕耳 - Because he heard the ancient Buddhist chants, he shed tears.
  • 谓曰汝见此间事到人间一一话之 - He said, 'Do you see these events here? Tell them to people.'
  • 当令世人闻之改心修善 - So that people will hear and change their hearts to do good.
  • 汝此生无杂行常正念 - In this life, you have no mixed actions and always maintain right mindfulness.
  • 可复来此 - You can come back here again.
  • 因令诸僧送归思元 - Therefore, he ordered the monks to send Siyuan back.
  • 初苏具三十人食别具二人肉食 - Initially, he recovered and prepared food for thirty people, separately preparing meat dishes for two people.
  • 皆有赠盖由此也 - All had gifts, which was due to this reason.
  • 思元活七日又设大斋毕思元又死 - Siyuan lived for seven days, then another grand feast was held, and Siyuan died again.
  • 至晓苏云向又为菩萨所召怒思元曰 - By morning, he recovered and said, 'I was again summoned by the Bodhisattva and became angry with Siyuan.'
  • 吾令汝具宣报应事何不言之 - I ordered you to fully explain the consequences, why didn't you say it?
  • 将杖之思元哀请乃放 - I was going to punish you, but after Siyuan pleaded, I let him go.
  • 而其家尽不过中食而思元每人集处必具言防中事人皆化之焉 - And his family did not exceed midday meals, while Siyuan, wherever he gathered, always spoke of the events in the realm, and people were all transformed by it.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 菩萨 - Refers to a Bodhisattva, a being who has achieved enlightenment but chooses to remain in the world to help others achieve enlightenment.
  • 梵音 - Refers to the ancient Buddhist chants, which are often associated with spiritual and moral guidance.
  • 大斋 - Refers to a grand feast, often held in religious contexts to celebrate significant events or to honor deities.
  • 长斋 - Refers to a long-term vegetarian diet, often practiced by devout Buddhists as a form of spiritual discipline.
  • 思元 - The name of a person, likely a devout follower of Buddhism who has undergone significant spiritual experiences.

Proper Nouns

  • 菩萨 - The Bodhisattva, a central figure in Buddhist tradition.

  • 大斋 - A grand feast, often held in religious contexts.
  • 长斋 - A long-term vegetarian diet, often practiced by devout Buddhists.

[1248]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遂棰朴交至死于堂下 - Classical Chinese phrase meaning 'he was beaten to death in the hall.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 地藏菩萨 - Major Buddhist deity in East Asian traditions, associated with saving souls from hell; rendered as 'Bodhisattva Dizang' or 'Bodhisattva Ksitigarbha.'
  • 僧戒 - Monastic rules of conduct in Buddhism; translated as 'monastic precepts' or 'Buddhist vows.'
  • 闲静寺 - A temple name or generic term for a secluded, quiet place of worship; rendered as 'Quiet Temple' or 'Secluded Temple.'
  • 等身像 - A statue of the same height as the person it represents; translated as 'life-sized statue' or 'statue of one’s own size.'

Proper Nouns

  • 王 - Title; rendered as 'the King.'
  • 寺主 - Title; rendered as 'the temple master' or 'the head of the temple.'
  • 马师 - Proper name; rendered as 'Ma Shi.'
  • 青衣 - Proper noun; rendered as 'the green-robed servant' or 'the servant in green robes.'
  • 纪闻 - Source title; rendered as 'from *Jiwen*.'

[1249]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天宝十二载冬 - In the twelfth year of Tianbao winter (天宝 is the era name of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang).
  • 其二人一则无是妻一则同曲富叟王翁 - Among them were two people: one was Zhang Wu Shi's wife, and the other was a wealthy old man named Wang Gong from the same street.
  • 无是闻之大惊俄而取者至云诸人尽得唯无是妻诵金刚经善神护之故不得 - Zhang Wu Shi heard this and was greatly alarmed. Soon, the fetchers arrived and said that all the others were taken, except Zhang Wu Shi's wife, who was protected by a virtuous deity because she was reciting the *Diamond Sutra*.
  • 因唱所得人名皆应曰唯无是亦识王翁应声答曰毕 - They then called out the names of those they had taken, and everyone responded 'yes,' including Zhang Wu Shi, who also recognized Wang Gong, who answered 'done'.
  • 鼓动无是归家见其妻犹诵经坐待 - When the drum signaled the end of the night, Zhang Wu Shi returned home and saw his wife still reciting the sutra, waiting for him.
  • 天晓闻南邻哭声无是问之则王翁死矣 - When dawn came, he heard crying from the south neighbor. He asked and learned that Wang Gong had died.
  • 无是大惧因以具告其妻妻亦大惧因移出宅谒名僧发誓愿长斋日则诵经四十九遍由是得免 - Zhang Wu Shi was greatly afraid and told his wife everything. His wife was also greatly afraid and moved out of the house to seek refuge with a famous monk, making vows and fasting daily, reciting the *Diamond Sutra* 49 times, thereby avoiding the danger.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天宝十二载 - The twelfth year of the Tianbao era, which corresponds to AD 753 during the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.

  • 布政坊 - A district in Chang'an, the capital city of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 金刚经 - The *Diamond Sutra*, a Buddhist scripture known for its teachings on emptiness and wisdom.
  • 名僧 - A famous monk, indicating a respected religious figure.
  • 长斋 - Fasting, often associated with religious or spiritual practices.
  • 诵经四十九遍 - Reciting a sutra 49 times, a common practice in Buddhist rituals for purification or protection.

Proper Nouns

  • 布政坊 - Bu Zheng Fang, a district in Chang'an.
  • 金刚经 - The *Diamond Sutra*, a Buddhist scripture.
  • 王翁 - Wang Gong, a wealthy old man from the same street.
  • 名僧 - A famous monk, indicating a respected religious figure.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence is elliptical and requires understanding that it refers to the dismissal of Buddhist monks who explained Buddhist scriptures and the request for the expulsion of those who do not follow proper teachings.
  • This long sentence describes a sequence of events: the imperial decree was about to be carried out, the kitchen officials were preparing the emperor's meals, chicken eggs were being cooked in a large vessel, and just as the fire was lit beneath it, a very faint sound like the voice of a person was heard inside the vessel, which turned out to be a group of eggs calling out to the Bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara, with the sound being very sad and choked, as if they were making a complaint.
  • This sentence describes Emperor Wenzong's reaction and subsequent actions: he sighed and said he did not know that the power of Buddhism is indeed like this, and the next day, he ordered the official in charge of the imperial kitchen not to use chicken eggs for the meal and issued an edict to the various regions and states, ordering each to sculpt an image of the Bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara in their temples.

  • 浮屠氏 - An archaic term for Buddhism.
  • 精舍 - A temple or place of worship.

  • 以火烬吾尸 - Command to cremate the body. '烬' (to burn to ashes) is poetic and should be rendered accordingly.
  • 支体连贯若纫缀之状 - Metaphor describing the body parts as if sewn together. '纫缀' is a poetic expression meaning 'sewn' or 'stitched.'
  • 纎韵徐引 - Likely describing the sound of wind through the remains, possibly a poetic or symbolic reference. '纎韵' may mean 'delicate melody' or 'fine sound.'
  • 里人竞施金钱 - '里人' refers to the local people or villagers. '竞施金钱' means they competed to offer money.
  • 建一塔以居士鏁瘗于塔中 - '鏁' is a rare or variant character, possibly a corruption or variant of '骨' (bones), or a specific term for the remains; render with note of uncertainty.
  • 大风黑云覆寺 - Dramatic scene: 'a great wind and black clouds covered the temple.' Typical metaphor in classical Chinese for divine or supernatural intervention.
  • 云中火起电击门楼 - 'Fire arose in the clouds, lightning struck the gate tower.' Vivid description of a supernatural event.
  • 未施丹艧而楼势东倾 - '未施丹艧' means 'before being painted with vermilion lacquer.' '楼势东倾' describes the tower tilting to the east.
  • 以大木撑之 - 'With a large beam to prop it up.' Technical construction term.
  • 葢神以像故而共扶持焉 - 'It was the ghosts and spirits, because of the image, that together supported it.' Supernatural explanation.

  • 宣室志 - Classical Chinese text, possibly a collection of anecdotes or stories; rendered as 'Xuanshi Zhi' (The Records of the Hall of Illumination) with note that it is a classical Chinese text.
  • 纪闻 - Term for 'collected accounts' or 'recorded events'; translated as 'Jiwen' or 'Collected Accounts.'
  • 鲁郡任城野黄山 - Geographical reference. '鲁郡' is a historical region in present-day Shandong Province. '任城' is a county within it. '黄山' is a mountain.
  • 瑞像 - '瑞' means 'auspicious' or 'divine,' and '像' is an image or statue. '瑞像' refers to a sacred or miraculous image.
  • 采梠者 - 'Cai' means to gather or collect, and 'Li' is a type of wild plant or food. '采梠者' refers to someone who gathers wild food.
  • 大斋 - Buddhist term for a large vegetarian meal or communal fasting event; translated as 'a great vegetarian feast' or 'a grand fasting ceremony.'

  • 为人坎轲 - Classical expression meaning 'life has been full of hardship and misfortune.'
  • 精持内教 - '内教' refers to the internal or native religion, likely Buddhism. '精持' means 'to practice diligently.'
  • 西方业成 - '西方' is the 'Western Paradise' (Sukhavati), a concept in Pure Land Buddhism. '业成' means 'karmic work completed.'
  • 往生安乐世界 - '往生' is a term used in Pure Land Buddhism for rebirth in the Western Paradise, often translated as 'to be reborn in the Pure Land' or 'to attain rebirth in the Land of Bliss.'
  • 念佛 - A core Buddhist practice, meaning 'to recite the name of Amitābha Buddha' or simply 'to chant the Buddha's name.'
  • 斋天 - A Buddhist ritual involving fasting and offerings to the heavens, often performed on special occasions.
  • 合掌 - A gesture of reverence or prayer, often translated as 'to press palms together' or 'to make the prayer gesture.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 泾县尉 - A local official (county magistrate) in the county of Jing, during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 丁忧 - A traditional Confucian practice where officials had to resign from their posts and go into mourning for a parent’s death, typically for 27 months.
  • 租纲 - Refers to a tax or tribute system, particularly grain shipments to the capital.
  • 天宝十年 - A year in the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang (751 CE).
  • 南陵山寺 - A Buddhist monastery located in Nanling Mountain.
  • 大中末 - The end of the Dazhong era, a reign period of Emperor Xuanzong’s successor, Emperor Wenzong of Tang (859 CE).
  • 圣画堂 - A hall containing sacred or holy paintings, likely of the Buddha or Bodhisattvas.
  • 菩萨摩其疮 - 'A Bodhisattva touched his wound.' This is a typical narrative in Buddhist hagiography.
  • 立社建堂 - To establish a community (society) and build a hall or temple in honor of the deity.
  • 释迦生中国设教如周孔 - 'Shakyamuni was born in China and taught the Dharma just as Confucius and Laozi did.' This is a comparative statement that reflects the syncretic attitude of some Tang-era texts toward Buddhism and Confucianism.
  • 天堂无则已有则君子登地狱无则已有则小人入 - A philosophical statement suggesting that if a heavenly realm exists, then virtuous people ascend to it; if a hell exists, then wicked people descend to it. It reflects a dualistic cosmology common in Buddhist and Daoist texts.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '裴宣礼天后朝为地官侍郎常持金刚经坐事被系宣礼忧迫唯至心念经枷锁一旦自脱推官亲访之遂得雪免御史任植同禁亦念经获免' describes the imprisonment of Pei Xuanli, his distress, his sincere recitation of the Diamond Sutra, the miraculous release of his shackles, and the subsequent exoneration by the court. The sentence is elliptical and requires careful segmentation to ensure clarity.
  • The sentence '吴思玄天后朝为太学博士信释氏持金刚经日两遍多有灵应后稍怠日夜一遍思玄在京病有巫褚细儿言事如神星下祈祷思玄往就见细儿惊曰公有何术鬼见皆走思玄私访知是经力倍加精励日念五遍兄疾医无效思玄至心念经三日而愈思玄曾于渭桥见一老人年八十余着粗缞服问之曰为所生母也思玄怪之答曰母年四十三时有异僧教云汝欲长寿否但念金刚经母即发心日念两遍终一百七姨邻母亦诵之并过百岁思玄遵母业日念两遍今九十矣' describes Wu Sixuan's imprisonment, his distress, his sincere recitation of the Diamond Sutra, the miraculous release of his shackles, and the subsequent exoneration by the court. The sentence is elliptical and requires careful segmentation to ensure clarity.

  • The sequence of actions in '虚见饮处意不能忍行伫立观之' is compressed and needs to be expanded for clarity.
  • The description of the demon's action in '牛头狱卒出于牀下以叉刺之洞胸' is vivid but requires careful translation to maintain the imagery.
  • The sequence of the character's actions in '虚遽连饮数杯乃出' is rapid and needs to be clearly articulated.
  • The character's traits and actions in '李虚素性凶顽不知罪福而被酒违戾' are described in a compressed manner and need to be expanded for clarity.
  • The sequence of events and their implications in '身得生天火焚罪簿获福若此非为善之报乎' need to be clearly articulated.
  • The contrast between diligent good deeds and the cycle of life and death in '与夫日夜精勤孜孜为善既持僧律常行佛言而不离生死未之有也' needs to be clearly articulated.

He is then sent back home, and as a result, his family and descendants begin to practice Buddhism, hang banners, and recite the Lotus Sutra to accumulate merit.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 迎飚截流 - A metaphorical expression that combines natural phenomena with supernatural elements, common in Chinese religious and literary traditions.
  • 牵挽之者 - Implies supernatural assistance, reflecting the belief in divine intervention in Chinese culture.
  • 迳到其岸 - Emphasizes the miraculous nature of the event, common in religious and folk tales.
  • 一舫全济 - Highlights the miraculous outcome, reinforcing the theme of divine protection and intervention.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 释慧和 - A Buddhist monk from the Southern Song dynasty; translated as 'Venerable Huhe' or 'Monk Huhe.'
  • 元嘉之难 - Refers to the Yuanjia Rebellion (453 CE), a major conflict during the Southern Song dynasty; translated as 'the Yuanjia disaster' or 'the Yuanjia upheaval.'
  • 刘胡部下 - Liu Hu was a general in the Southern Song, and this refers to his subordinates; translated as 'under General Liu Hu.'
  • 观世音 - The Bodhisattva Guanyin, a central figure in Mahayana Buddhism; rendered as 'Guanyin' or 'Avalokiteśvara' depending on the target audience.

  • 感应传 - A Buddhist text on miraculous events; kept in transliteration with a note: *Gan Ying Zhuan* (Book of Responsive Miracles).
  • 辨正论 - Another Buddhist text; kept as *Bian Zheng Lun* (Discourse on Correcting Errors).
  • 齐建安王 - A prince of the Southern Qi dynasty; rendered as 'Prince Jian’an of Qi.'
  • 虎牢 - A fortress in ancient China, now known as Hulao Pass; translated as 'Hulao Pass.'

[1404]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 像幡座俨然如故像于中夜大放赤光 - Describes a statue or image that remains unchanged and emits a red light at midnight. '像幡' refers to a banner or standard with an image, often used in religious or ceremonial contexts. '俨然如故' means 'as if unchanged' or 'unchanged in appearance.' '大放赤光' means 'to emit a bright red light.'
  • 其时诸祭酒有二十许人有惧灵验宻委去者 - Describes a situation where twenty or so scholars (祭酒) were present, and some were afraid of the supernatural phenomena and secretly left. '诸祭酒' refers to a group of scholars or learned men. '有惧灵验' means 'fearing the supernatural phenomena.' '宻委去者' means 'secretly left.'
  • 巨等师徒意犹不止被发禹步报持刀索云斥佛还故国不得留中夏为民害也 - Describes the actions of the remaining scholars, who continued to perform rituals and curse the Buddha, demanding that it return to its original country and not stay in China, as it was causing harm. '巨等师徒' refers to the remaining scholars and their disciples. '意犹不止' means 'still continuing.' '被发禹步' is a ritual dance performed by shamans or sorcerers. '报持刀索' means 'brandishing knives and ropes.' '斥佛还故国' means 'to curse the Buddha to return to its original country.' '不得留中夏为民害也' means 'it should not stay in China and cause harm to the people.'
  • 龄于其处如有人殴打顿仆于地家人扶起方余气息遂痿躄不能行 - Describes a person who was beaten and fell to the ground, and was only able to be revived by their family, but subsequently became paralyzed and unable to walk. '龄于其处' means 'at that place.' '如有人殴打' means 'as if someone was beating.' '顿仆于地' means 'fell to the ground.' '家人扶起方余气息' means 'the family revived them just as they were barely breathing.' '遂痿躄不能行' means 'and subsequently became paralyzed and unable to walk.'
  • 魏巨体内发疽日出血三升不一月苦死自外同伴并患癞疾邻人东安太守水立和传于东阳时多见者 - Describes the subsequent events, where the person who was beaten developed a severe infection, bled heavily, and died within a month. Their companions also contracted leprosy, and the news was spread by the local governor to the nearby city of Dongyang, where many people witnessed the events. '魏巨体内发疽' means 'the person developed a severe infection inside their body.' '日出血三升' means 'bled three liters of blood per day.' '不一月苦死' means 'died within a month.' '自外同伴并患癞疾' means 'their companions also contracted leprosy.' '邻人东安太守水立和传于东阳' means 'the local governor of Dongan, Shui Lihe, spread the news to Dongyang.' '时多见者' means 'many people witnessed the events.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 像幡座俨然如故像于中夜大放赤光 - This phrase likely refers to a religious or ceremonial statue that emitted a red light at midnight, which was seen as a supernatural event. Such phenomena were often interpreted as omens or signs from the divine.
  • 其时诸祭酒有二十许人有惧灵验宻委去者 - The term '祭酒' refers to scholars or learned men, often associated with Confucianism. The fear of supernatural phenomena and the subsequent departure of some scholars suggest a context where religious or supernatural events were causing concern among the educated elite.
  • 巨等师徒意犹不止被发禹步报持刀索云斥佛还故国不得留中夏为民害也 - The performance of rituals and the cursing of the Buddha indicate a context of religious conflict, possibly between Buddhism and other religious traditions. The demand for the Buddha to return to its 'original country' suggests a belief that Buddhism was foreign and potentially harmful to Chinese society.
  • 龄于其处如有人殴打顿仆于地家人扶起方余气息遂痿躄不能行 - The description of a person being beaten and subsequently becoming paralyzed suggests a violent event, possibly related to the religious conflict. The paralysis could be interpreted as a supernatural punishment or a result of the physical trauma.
  • 魏巨体内发疽日出血三升不一月苦死自外同伴并患癞疾邻人东安太守水立和传于东阳时多见者 - The development of a severe infection and the subsequent death, along with the contraction of leprosy by companions, suggest a context of disease and suffering. The spread of the news by the local governor indicates that the events were significant enough to be reported to a wider audience.

Proper Nouns

  • 祭酒 - Refers to scholars or learned men, often associated with Confucianism.
  • 佛 - Refers to the Buddha, the central figure of Buddhism.
  • 中夏 - Refers to China, often used in historical texts to denote the Chinese civilization.
  • 东安太守水立和 - Refers to the local governor of Dongan, Shui Lihe, who spread the news to Dongyang.
  • 东阳 - Refers to the city of Dongyang, where the events were witnessed by many people.
